Meet Thesis Advisor

Hi everyone!

I’m Munus.

Are you my student?

I teach a wide range of classes…

Tech A is a course that teaches about how designers how to think computationally
Tech A — tech-a.munusshih.com ↗
Thesis I — thesis-i.munusshih.com ↗
Thesis II — thesis-ii.munusshih.com ↗
Visual Language B that challenges us to imagine collective ways of working that moves beyond the current caplitstic structure.
vlb.munusshih.com ↗

My work ethos

I love to make my own tools

I believe that making your own open source design tool is the first step to build a resilient practice without relying on proprietary software.

p5.zine is a tool for making an interactive and generative zine with p5.js
p5.zine — openprocessing.org ↗
A tool for creating books with code. It can export to PDF with saddle stitch and bleed settings.
p5.book — editor.p5js.org ↗

I was able to produce all the design for the AIGA 50 Books x 50 Covers Exhibition this semester using my own custom tool. Another alternative is possible!

In collaboration with Gaia Hwang, Marily Papanastasatou, Ananya Soogoor and Yashasyaa Arya
AIGA 50 Books x 50 Covers Exhibition — www.munusshih.com ↗

professional practice

Selected Projects


I work with many mission-aligned organizations and independent researchers to build design systems and digital work.

  • AIGA
    at Athletics
    Image of the Studio — imageofthestudio.athleticsnyc.io ↗

    at Athletics

    with Nikki Makagiansar
    The Only Thing That Lasts — blackburnoralhistoryproject.org ↗
  • Just Tech Fellow
    with Nikki Makagiansar
    Unearthing Air — todd-dev.free99.work ↗

    at Athletics

    with Nikki Makagiansar

  • New York Review of Books
  • Asian Labor Future Institute
  • Black Zine Fair

I also do...

Research & Exhibitions

I collaborate with other artists and researchers to produce exhibitions, workshops, and publications. My research focuses on open source ecosystem, experimental publishing, and solidarity economy.

Collaborated with Tee Topor and Nikki Makagiansar, a time-based store
Duty Free — www.munusshih.com ↗
Collaborated with Nikki Makagiansar, Franco Chen, Em DeVincentis, Showcased at NEW INC x Demo Festival 2025
Syllabus (Subject to Change) — www.munusshih.com ↗
Conversation between Anoushka Bhalla, Alexandria Deters and Munus Shih, showcased at Accent Sisters
Threads of Memory — www.munusshih.com ↗
Collaborated with Ihao Liao and Chun-Huang Lin
Practicing Ecological Distance — ntu01.jpg ↗

I run a cooperative

Worker-owned!

with my two best friends Tzuyun Wei and Ihao Liao!

design co-op
CoAssembly — sponact-1.jpg ↗
Art Bookfair in Taipei
PCD Taiwan — sponact-2.jpg ↗
new book!
Recent Publication — sponact-3.png ↗
new exhibition at National Taiwan University!
Recent Publication — sponact-4.png ↗

However, thesis is not about me...

Thesis is about You!

Here are some things I’m familiar with…

Open Source Experimental Publishing Digital Toolmaking Organizing Togetherness World-building Mutual Aid Critical Code Poetic Computation Open Data Decolonizing Technology Design Justice Archival Practice Building Database Speculative Design Generative Design Critical Pedagogy Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning Natural Language Processing Data Visualization

But I’m also interested in learning about your interests, your practice, and your thesis project. I want to support you in making the work that you want to make, not the work that I want you to make.

We will work well if...

We will work well together if…

You're a book lover, I've been diving into this book by Mark Fisher's lectures on capitalism. I read many books as part of my research and I want to share them with you!
Postcapitalist Desire — kyl.neocities.org ↗
You love archive, index and collection. This work by Nora Wang archives nostalgic commercial products in China and the authentic memories associated with them in the age of globalization and capitalism.
Nora (Huixin) Wang — nora.png ↗
You investigate systems and power! Jenelle Zhao's work ____topia is speculative storytelling kit that invites users to imagine and build alternative worlds that rejects the common dystopian and utopian narratives and instead embraces the complexity and contradictions of our world.
Jenelle (Wenjing) Zhao — Jenelle.jpeg ↗
You love preso and crits! We will have regular preso and crits sessions to share our work and get feedback from each other. I will also invite guest critics to join us and provide feedback on your work.
crits — preso.JPG ↗
You are experimental! I will support you in making the work that you want to make, not the work that I want you to make. I will also encourage you to experiment with different mediums, formats, and tools to find the best way to express your ideas.
Me with Student Books — books.jpg ↗

No pressure!

It’s just another project!

Email me at munus.shih@pratt.edu

Or find me in the hallways of Pratt!
